zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Charles Karel Murray於1415年出生在英國蘇格蘭度福利爾考普爾時，他的父親Sir Thomas Murray33歲，母親Jean Scott32歲。他和Lady Margaret Erskine至少有3個兒子。他去世於1470年在他的家鄉享年55歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Charles Karel Murray于1415年出生在英国苏格兰度福利爾考普爾时，他的父亲Sir Thomas Murray33岁，母亲Jean Scott32岁。他和Lady Margaret Erskine至少有3个儿子。他去世于1470年在他的家乡享年55岁。

nameMeanings.name: Murray
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Moray in northeastern Scotland, which derives from Celtic mori- ‘sea’ + treb- ‘settlement’. The founder of the Scottish house of Murray was a Fleming named Freskin who was granted Strathbrock in West Lothian and Duffus in Moray by David I. The family took its name from the region in the late 12th century.
Irish and Scottish: shortened form of   McMurray  .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Muireadhaigh ‘descendant of Muireadhach’ a personal name meaning ‘mariner’. Occasionally it may be a shortened form of McMurray .

variants.name: Murry
meanings: Scottish and Irish: variant of   Murray  .

variants.name: Murrah
meanings: Probably an altered form of Scottish   Murray  .

variants.name: Murie
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Murie in Errol (Perthshire).
Scottish and Irish: variant of   Murray  .

variants.name: Merhi
meanings: Arabic (mainly Lebanon and Syria): from a variant of the personal name Mirhi of unknown meaning. Bearers of this surname are both Muslims and Christians. Compare Murray 5.

variants.name: McMurray
meanings: Irish (northern) and Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Muireadhaigh or Mac Muireadhaich, a patronymic from the personal name Muireadhach ‘seafarer’ (see Murdoch ). Compare McCurry 1.

variants.name: Murra
meanings: Italian (Sicily): topographic name from Sicilian murra ‘large rock, mass of rocks’ or a habitational name from a place called with this word.
Albanian: nickname from murrë (definite form murra) ‘dark gray cow’.

nameMeanings.name: Charles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Germanic word, karl, meaning ‘free man’, akin to Old English ceorl ‘man’. The name, Latin form Carolus, owed its popularity in medieval Europe to the Frankish leader Charlemagne ( ?742–814 ), who in 800 established himself as Holy Roman Emperor. His name (Latin Carolus Magnus) means ‘Charles the Great’. Carolus—or Karl, the German form—was a common name among Frankish leaders, including Charlemagne's grandfather Charles Martel ( 688–741 ). Charles is the French form. The name occurs occasionally in medieval Britain as Karolus or Carolus; it had a certain vogue in West Yorkshire from the 1400s, particularly among gentry families. The form Charles was chosen by Mary Queen of Scots ( 1542–87 ), who had been brought up in France, for her son, Charles James ( 1566–1625 ), who became King James VI of Scotland and, from 1603 , James I of England. His son and grandson both reigned as King Charles , and the name thus became established in the 17th century both in the Stuart royal house and among English and Scottish supporters of the Stuart monarchy. In the 18th century it was to some extent favoured, along with James , by Jacobites, supporters of the exiled Stuarts, opposed to the Hanoverian monarchy, especially in the Highlands of Scotland. In the 19th century the popularity of the name was further enhanced by romanticization of the story of ‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’, leader of the 1745 rebellion.

nameMeanings.name: Karel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Germanic word, karl, meaning ‘free man’, akin to Old English ceorl ‘man’. The name, Latin form Carolus, owed its popularity in medieval Europe to the Frankish leader Charlemagne ( ?742–814 ), who in 800 established himself as Holy Roman Emperor. His name (Latin Carolus Magnus) means ‘Charles the Great’. Carolus—or Karl, the German form—was a common name among Frankish leaders, including Charlemagne's grandfather Charles Martel ( 688–741 ). Charles is the French form. The name occurs occasionally in medieval Britain as Karolus or Carolus; it had a certain vogue in West Yorkshire from the 1400s, particularly among gentry families. The form Charles was chosen by Mary Queen of Scots ( 1542–87 ), who had been brought up in France, for her son, Charles James ( 1566–1625 ), who became King James VI of Scotland and, from 1603 , James I of England. His son and grandson both reigned as King Charles , and the name thus became established in the 17th century both in the Stuart royal house and among English and Scottish supporters of the Stuart monarchy. In the 18th century it was to some extent favoured, along with James , by Jacobites, supporters of the exiled Stuarts, opposed to the Hanoverian monarchy, especially in the Highlands of Scotland. In the 19th century the popularity of the name was further enhanced by romanticization of the story of ‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’, leader of the 1745 rebellion.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Charles Karel Murray was born in 1415, in Cockpool, Dumfriesshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Thomas Murray, was 33 and his mother, Jean Scott, was 32. He had at least 3 sons with Lady Margaret Erskine. He died in 1470, in his hometown, at the age of 55.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Charles Karel Murray est né en 1415, à Cockpool, Dumfriesshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Thomas Murray, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Jean Scott, avait 32 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils avec Lady Margaret Erskine. Il est décédé en 1470, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 55 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Charles Karel Murray 1415 in Cockpool, Dumfriesshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Thomas Murray 33 und seine Mutter Jean Scott 32. Er hatte mit Lady Margaret Erskine mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb 1470 im Alter von 55 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Charles Karel Murray è nato il nel 1415 nel Cockpool, Dumfriesshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir Thomas Murray, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Jean Scott, aveva 32 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli con Lady Margaret Erskine. Morì nel 1470, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 55 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Charles Karel Murrayが1415年にイギリススコットランドダムフリーシュアイアコクプオオルで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas Murrayは33歳，母Jean Scottは32歳であった。彼はLady Margaret Erskineとの間に少なくとも3人の息子をもうけた。彼は1470年に故郷で55歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Charles Karel Murray(이)가 1415년 영국 스코틀랜드 드프리실르 콕포에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Thomas Murray(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Jean Scott(은)는 32세였다. 그는 Lady Margaret Erskine(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1470년 그의 고향에서 55세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Charles Karel Murray nasceu em 1415, em Cockpool, Dumfriesshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Thomas Murray, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Jean Scott, tinha 32 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos com Lady Margaret Erskine. Ele faleceu em 1470, em sua cidade natal, com 55 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Charles Karel Murray родился в 1415 г. в местности коккпул, думфриессхир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Thomas Murray было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Jean Scott, 32. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына от Lady Margaret Erskine. Он умер в 1470 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 55 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Charles Karel Murray nació en 1415, en Cockpool, Dumfriesshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Thomas Murray, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Jean Scott, tenía 32 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos con Lady Margaret Erskine. Murió en 1470, en su pueblo, a la edad de 55 años.
